Clark is Counsel at Fragomen’s Salt Lake City office, where he advises corporate clients on U.S. immigration and nationality law, policy and regulatory compliance. He has extensive experience with employment- and family-based immigration, including PERM labor certification, immigrant and nonimmigrant visas and adjustment of status. Clark represents employers and individuals before the U.S. Department of Labor, U.S. Department of Homeland Security and the U.S. Department of State in immigration matters.
Prior to joining Fragomen, Clark practiced U.S. immigration law at a large business immigration firm, where he managed the inbound Canada practice and was an integral part of the firm’s global immigration and account management team. He advised multinational companies in a variety of industries, including energy, healthcare, financial services, engineering, manufacturing and information technology, on strategies and best practices for developing and executing global mobility programs.
During law school, Clark was a Judicial Law Clerk Intern with the Executive Office for Immigration Review and a Legal Intern with the Office of the Chief Counsel for Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
- University of Miami School of Law, J.D., 2012
- University of Utah, B.S., 2006
Bar Admissions: Utah
Practice Limited to Federal Immigration & Nationality Law
Listed in Chambers USA , 2024 to 2025
Named to Utah Business Legal Elite, 2024 to 2025

Member, Global Business Travel Association, Utah Chapter

Adjunct Professor, University of Utah S.J. Quinney College of Law

Former Member, AILA Law Student Engagement Committee

### Publications and lectures
Speaker, “Top 5 Immigration Considerations for Legal Departments of Tech Companies.” Association of Corporate Counsel Mountain West 2025 Tech Law Symposium , Lehi, UT, 21 March 2025.
Panelist, “Navigating a Career in Structural Engineering as a Non-US Citizen.” Structural Engineers Association of Northern California (SEAONC) , Webinar, 11 March 2025.
Speaker, “Executive Orders, Immigration Enforcement, and Uncertainty: What the New Administration Means for Global Companies and Talent.” Fragomen , Denver, CO, 6 March 2025.
Panelist, “Immigration Panel.” Utah Mobility Leaders Quarterly Meeting , Salt Lake City, UT, 5 March 2025.
Speaker, “Immigration Industry Updates.” Idaho Mobility Group Winter Meeting , Boise, ID, 26 February 2025.
Speaker, “Utah Tech Week | Hiring International Employees Workshop.” Fragomen , Salt Lake City, UT, 29 January 2025.
Speaker, “Impact of a Second Trump Administration on Employment-Based Immigration: What to Expect, How to Prepare & Benchmarking.” Fragomen , Webinar, 18 November 2024.
Speaker, “Global Mobility Executive Denver Roundtable.” Global Mobility Executive Leaders Exchange, Denver, Colorado, 11 October 2023.
Speaker, “Global Mobility Executive Salt Lake City Roundtable.” Global Mobility Executive Leaders Exchange , Salt Lake City, Utah, 10 October 2023.
Panelist, “Student Immigration Law Association Panel.” University of Utah S.J. Quinney College of Law , Salt Lake City, Utah, 14 September 2023.
Speaker, “Basics of U.S. Employment-Based Immigration.” Utah State Bar , Salt Lake City, Utah, 24 April 2023.
Speaker, “H-1B Visas to Attract and Retain Top Talent.” Fragomen , Webinar, 14 December 2021.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
